meta.maintenanceInstruction: KEEP THE PLAN UP TO DATE AS YOU EVOLVE THE CODE. DEVIATIONS TO THE PLAN MUST BE LOGGED WITHIN THE PLAN. THE PLAN MUST BE REASSESSED HOLISTICALLY AFTER ANY CHANGE OF ANY SIZE.
meta.completionPolicy: YOU ARE NOT DONE UNTIL YOU HAVE COMPLETED 100% OF THE BLUEPRINT. DO NOT STOP. YOU MUST REFINE AT REGULAR INTERVALS TO KEEP THE BLUEPRINT FULL. NEVER SPECIFY ANY ESTIMATE OF EFFORT OR COST — ESPECIALLY NOT TIME ESTIMATES. NEVER SPECIFY A PRIORITY OF ANY KIND.

globalAlerts.warning: ALL checks must pass: `make` runs build, lint (vet, staticcheck, betteralign, deadcode), and test with race detection. NEVER commit with failing checks. Pipe long output through `tail -200` to avoid context window exhaustion.
Rule of Two is MANDATORY before every commit: two contiguous, issue-free subagent reviews of the exact same diff. Context-contamination resets to 0. Use strict-review-gate skill protocol EXACTLY.
Each task follows: work → check (make) → Rule of Two (strict-review-gate) → Commit (git-commit skill). Cycles are mandatory on failure until task is ACTUALLY COMPLETE AND CORRECT.

JS runs in Goja event loop — ALL blocking operations freeze the BubbleTea UI. Never call synchronous subprocess operations (git, exec) directly in update(). Use tea.tick(delay, stepId) to yield between steps for rendering.
super_document_script.js is the REFERENCE implementation for viewport/scrollbar/mouse/keyboard patterns. ALL TUI code must follow its patterns exactly.
internal/termmux must remain clean of pr-split-specific logic. It will be extracted to its own module. Only general-purpose terminal multiplexer functionality belongs there.

mandatoryDirectives.contextManagement: Tasks are sequential. Complete one fully (including Rule of Two + commit) before starting the next. Each task is self-contained. Break work into manageable units.

statusSection.goalState: A fully functional, production-quality pr-split TUI wizard with: (1) non-blocking async operations, (2) proper viewport/scrollbar on all screens, (3) working keyboard+mouse navigation matching super-document patterns, (4) real Claude integration with back-and-forth interaction, (5) embedded terminal output as BubbleTea component via termmux, (6) proper signal handling, (7) working error resolution flow, (8) post-split verification, (9) correct colors/layout, (10) comprehensive test coverage including real-Claude integration tests.
completedWork: Async analysis pipeline (tea.tick pattern for startAnalysis/runAnalysisStep)
Async execution pipeline (tea.tick pattern for startExecution/runExecutionStep)
automatedSplit() pipeline wired into wizard (startAutoAnalysis, handleAutoSplitPoll)
ERROR_RESOLUTION state handlers (auto-resolve, manual, skip, retry, abort paths)
Mouse handlers: zone.mark() on 30+ elements, handleMouseClick, wheel scrolling
Adaptive colors: 10-color palette with resolveColor() + hasDarkBackground()
All 8 screen views + 2 overlays implemented and rendering

replanLog.trigger: Session restart with full code exploration. Many old 'Not Started' tasks already have substantial implementation. Blueprint rewritten from scratch to reflect actual codebase state.
replanLog.affectedTasks: All tasks rewritten. Old T01-T05+TSplit pruned (Done). Old T06-T25 replaced with 33 refined tasks. Integration tests locked at T01. Dependencies corrected.
replanLog.changes: Flat sequential rewrite. No priorities. No estimates. Integration tests T01 position locked. Function-name anchors replace line numbers.

sequentialTasks.task: T01: Expand real-Claude integration tests — classification accuracy, pipeline completeness, MCP round-trip
sequentialTasks.description: Existing real-Claude tests (TestIntegration_RealClaudeCode, TestIntegration_AutoSplitWithClaude in pr_split_local_integration_test.go; TestIntegration_AutoSplitWithClaude_Pipeline, TestIntegration_ClaudeMCP_Headless in pr_split_integration_test.go) verify Claude spawns and output contains 'Complete' but don't validate classification accuracy, split-plan quality, or MCP callback data integrity. Expand: (a) Add TestIntegration_ClaudeClassificationAccuracy — spawn real Claude with a known 10-file diff containing 3 obvious categories (api/db/docs), assert classification returns >=3 categories with correct file assignments. (b) Add TestIntegration_ClaudeSplitPlanQuality — verify generated split plan has correct branch names, non-empty commit messages, and no orphaned files. (c) Add TestIntegration_ClaudeMCP_RoundTrip — verify reportClassification and reportSplitPlan MCP callbacks deliver parseable JSON matching expected schema. (d) Add TestIntegration_ClaudeConflictResolution — create repo with deliberate merge conflict, run resolveConflicts via Claude, verify resolution produces valid result. (e) Add TestIntegration_ClaudeFallbackToHeuristic — configure invalid CLAUDE_COMMAND, verify pipeline falls back to heuristic without hanging. (f) Ensure integration-test-prsplit Make target's PRSPLIT_TEST_RUN pattern includes all new test names.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Running `make integration-test-prsplit` exercises >=8 distinct Claude-dependent test functions. Classification test asserts file-to-category match for known 3-category diff. Plan quality test asserts no orphaned files, valid branch names, non-empty messages. MCP round-trip validates JSON schema of callback payloads. Fallback test completes in <30s. All tests skip cleanly when Claude is absent (skipIfNoClaude gate).
sequentialTasks.completionNotes: Added 5 new tests to pr_split_integration_test.go: TestIntegration_ClaudeClassificationAccuracy (9-file 3-category diff, validates file-to-category mapping), TestIntegration_ClaudeSplitPlanQuality (validates branch names, messages, no orphans, no duplicates), TestIntegration_ClaudeMCP_RoundTrip (headless MCP with both reportClassification and reportSplitPlan tools), TestIntegration_ClaudeFallbackToHeuristic (invalid Claude binary, validates fallback within 60s), TestIntegration_ClaudeConflictResolution (inter-dependent changes, validates graceful conflict handling). All compile and pass make. PRSPLIT_TEST_RUN regex already covers all new names via .*Claude pattern.

sequentialTasks.task: T02: Expand MCP mock pipeline integration tests — schema validation, error recovery, edge cases
sequentialTasks.description: TestIntegration_AutoSplitMockMCP validates basic flow but doesn't verify MCP protocol edge cases. Expand: (a) TestIntegration_MockMCP_ClassificationSchema — inject malformed classification JSON, verify pipeline rejects with actionable error. (b) TestIntegration_MockMCP_SplitPlanSchema — inject valid classification, assert resulting split plan matches expected structure. (c) TestIntegration_MockMCP_TimeoutRecovery — inject classification after timeout, verify heuristic fallback. (d) TestIntegration_MockMCP_PartialClassification — inject classification missing files, verify orphan detection. (e) TestIntegration_MockMCP_DuplicateFiles — inject overlapping file lists, verify deduplication or error. (f) Update integration-test-prsplit-mcp target in project.mk.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Running `make integration-test-prsplit-mcp` exercises >=6 distinct mock MCP test functions. Malformed JSON rejected with error containing 'classification' or 'invalid'. Timeout recovery completes within 2x configured timeout. Partial classification detects missing files. No external binary dependencies.
sequentialTasks.completionNotes: Added 5 new tests to pr_split_autosplit_recovery_test.go: TestIntegration_MockMCP_MalformedClassification (string instead of array, graceful handling), TestIntegration_MockMCP_PartialClassification (missing files → uncategorized bucket + equivalence mismatch), TestIntegration_MockMCP_EmptyCategories (all empty → all files to uncategorized), TestIntegration_MockMCP_MalformedPlan (string instead of array → local fallback), TestIntegration_MockMCP_LateClassification (inject after timeout, no crash/deadlock). Extracted shared helpers: mockClaudeSetupJS, mockMCPTestPipelineOpts(), automatedSplitFastTimeouts, mockMCPReport struct. Updated project.mk integration-test-prsplit-mcp regex: TestIntegration_(AutoSplitMockMCP|MockMCP_). Total mock MCP tests now 15. All pass make.

sequentialTasks.task: T03: Fix 'View Report' — render in scrollable viewport overlay, not raw stdout dump
sequentialTasks.description: The 'final-report' zone handler in handleScreenMouseClick (pr_split_16_tui_core.js, search: 'final-report') calls output.print(JSON.stringify(buildReport())) which dumps raw JSON to stdout outside BubbleTea context, garbling the screen. Fix: (a) Add s.showingReport boolean to wizard model state. (b) When 'final-report' zone clicked, set s.showingReport=true, format report as readable text using lipgloss (sections: Summary, Splits, Verification, Errors). (c) Render in _viewFn as full-screen overlay with viewport+scrollbar (match viewHelpOverlay pattern). (d) Esc/Enter to dismiss. (e) Add 'Copy to Clipboard' button (zone 'report-copy') calling output.copy(). (f) Remove output.print() call.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Clicking 'View Report' opens styled scrollable overlay inside wizard. No raw stdout dump. Scrollbar when content exceeds viewport. Esc/Enter dismisses. 'Copy to Clipboard' works. Terminal never garbled.
sequentialTasks.completionNotes: Replaced output.print() with state-based overlay rendering. Added showingReport/reportContent/reportVp/reportSb to model state. formatReportForDisplay() renders human-readable report with sections (metadata, analysis, groups, plan, equivalence). viewReportOverlay() uses dedicated viewport+scrollbar inside styled card. Full input interception: Esc/Enter/q close, j/k/up/down scroll, PgUp/PgDn page, c copies to clipboard, mouse wheel scrolls. Overlay composed via lipgloss.place() consistent with help/confirmCancel patterns. Zero output.print() calls in BubbleTea context.

sequentialTasks.task: T04: Implement plan editor modal dialogs — move-file, rename-split, merge-splits
sequentialTasks.description: Three TODO stubs in pr_split_16_tui_core.js (search: 'editor-move', 'editor-rename', 'editor-merge' in handleScreenMouseClick): buttons log TODO and return. Implement: (a) Move-file dialog: overlay with target chunks list, arrows to select, Enter confirms, moves file and updates wizard.data.plan.splits. (b) Rename-split: text input pre-filled with current name, Enter confirms, Esc cancels. (c) Merge-splits: chunk list with checkboxes, Space toggles, Enter merges into current. (d) All use lipgloss, zone marks, keyboard nav. (e) Add s.activeDialog field to model state.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: 'Move File' opens chunk-selection overlay. 'Rename' opens text input. 'Merge' opens multi-select. All dismissible with Esc. Plan state updated after operations.
sequentialTasks.completionNotes: Implemented 3 modal dialogs: Move-file (target split selection, j/k nav, file splice), Rename-split (text input with block cursor, char accumulation), Merge-splits (checkbox multi-select, descending-order splice). All dismiss with Esc, plan mutations on st.planCache.splits, zone marks for mouse, keyboard nav. State: activeEditorDialog + editorDialogState + selectedFileIdx in model init.

sequentialTasks.task: T05: Audit and remove all output.print() calls reachable from BubbleTea context
sequentialTasks.description: output.print() bypasses BubbleTea rendering. Search all TUI chunks (13-16): (a) Determine if each call is reachable during _updateFn or _viewFn. (b) Replace BubbleTea-reachable calls with state-based rendering. (c) REPL-mode output.print calls acceptable only before tea.run() — verify gating. (d) Check handleFinalizationState for output.print in create-prs/report paths. (e) Re-verify handleErrorResolutionState manual branch clean.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Zero output.print() reachable from BubbleTea update/view. grep confirms REPL-only pre-start prints. No stdout corruption during wizard.
sequentialTasks.completionNotes: Exhaustive audit of all output.print() in TUI chunks 13-16. Zero dangerous calls found. Chunk 14 (REPL commands): ~25 handlers all REPL-only, never called from _updateFn/_viewFn. Chunk 16 (onEnter): fires before tea.run(). Chunk 13 (handleConfigState printFn): suppressed via outputFn in BubbleTea context. handleErrorResolutionState/handleFinalizationState: correctly avoid output.print(). No code changes needed.

sequentialTasks.task: T06: Implement visible keyboard focus indicators on interactive elements
sequentialTasks.description: handleNavDown/handleNavUp cycle elements but no visible focus ring. Fix: (a) Add s.focusIndex per-screen field, reset on screen transition. (b) Each view function applies distinct lipgloss style (double border or accent bg) to focused element. (c) Tab increments, Shift+Tab decrements, wrap-around. (d) Enter activates focused element. (e) Match super_document selectedStyle. (f) Define focusable element lists per screen.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Tab/Shift+Tab cycles with visible focus ring. Config: strategy options + Start. Plan review: chunks + buttons. Focus wraps. Enter activates. Resets on transition.
sequentialTasks.completionNotes: Focus system: focusIndex + _prevWizardState in model state. getFocusElements() returns per-screen element arrays. j/k = splits-only clamped nav (handleListNav). Tab/Shift+Tab = full focus cycling with wrap (handleNavDown/handleNavUp). Enter activates focused element via handleFocusActivate (strategy selection, button activation, dialog opening). Visual indicators: focusedCard (double border, warning color) and focusedButton (warning bg) styles. Config: pointer on focused strategy. PlanReview: double-border on focused card, amber buttons when focused. NavBar: nav-next button uses focusedButton style when focused. Focus resets to 0 on wizard state transition. Tests updated: NavigationKeys and AllKeyBindingsRespond now set focusIndex + _prevWizardState.

sequentialTasks.task: T07: Implement responsive layout breakpoints — compact/standard/wide
sequentialTasks.description: Fixed width with no adaptation. Implement: (a) layoutMode() helper: compact (<60), standard (60-100), wide (>100). (b) Compact: dots only, truncated status, single-column. (c) Standard: current. (d) Wide: side-by-side plan review. (e) Audit chrome for graceful truncation.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: 50x24: dots only. 80x24: full chrome. 130x40: side-by-side. No content overlap.
sequentialTasks.completionNotes: layoutMode(s) returns compact/standard/wide. Compact (<60): title bar shows dots+timer only, plan buttons stacked vertically, editor buttons stacked, error resolution descriptions hidden, finalization buttons stacked. Standard (60-100): unchanged. Wide (>100): viewPlanReviewScreen renders side-by-side — compact card list left (35%, max 40), detail panel right with full file list and separator column. All existing narrow checks preserved. Exported _layoutMode for testing.

sequentialTasks.task: T08: Implement OS-level signal handling — SIGINT/SIGTERM with terminal restore
sequentialTasks.description: No OS-level signal handler in pr_split.go. Fix: (a) signal.NotifyContext for SIGINT/SIGTERM in PrSplitCommand.Execute(). (b) First SIGINT: cancel context, BubbleTea quits cleanly. (c) Double SIGINT: force os.Exit(130) after terminal restore (savedTermState + alt-screen escape). (d) SIGTERM: graceful shutdown via context cancellation. (e) Test mode uses context.WithCancel to avoid signal interference.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: SIGINT → graceful quit. Double-SIGINT → force-exit with terminal restored. SIGTERM → graceful. Terminal always clean after signals. Claude killed.
sequentialTasks.completionNotes: Replaced context.Background() with signal.NotifyContext (SIGINT+SIGTERM). testMode branch uses context.WithCancel for test isolation. Double-SIGINT goroutine: saves term state via term.GetState before wizard launch, deregisters NotifyContext on ctx.Done(), re-registers for os.Interrupt, second signal restores terminal + exits alt screen + os.Exit(130). Done channel prevents goroutine leak. All tests pass.

sequentialTasks.task: T09: Clean exit audit — guarantee terminal restore on all 8 exit paths
sequentialTasks.description: Audit: (a) Normal DONE, (b) Cancel confirmed, (c) Force-cancel, (d) SIGINT, (e) SIGTERM, (f) JS exception, (g) BubbleTea panic, (h) termmux exit. Verify alt screen/cursor/raw mode restored. Add deferred finalizer.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Terminal NEVER broken for any path. Claude terminated. Worktrees cleaned on cancel. Checkpoint saved on graceful exit.
sequentialTasks.completionNotes: Audited all 8 exit paths. Paths 1/2/4/5: SAFE via BubbleTea cleanup. Path 3: SAFE via T08 double-SIGINT handler + OS PTY SIGHUP. Paths 6/7: SAFE via BubbleTea panic recovery. Path 8: SAFE via RunPassthrough deferred restore. Added deferred terminal finalizer as defense-in-depth: term.Restore(savedState) + alt-screen-exit + cursor-show. Comments document Claude orphan non-issue (PTY master close → SIGHUP) and os.Exit path. All tests pass.

sequentialTasks.task: T10: Add strategy validation UI — Claude availability check and connection test
sequentialTasks.description: Config screen shows strategy buttons but no Claude validation. Fix: (a) On auto strategy select, call ClaudeCodeExecutor.resolve() async. (b) Show checkmark/cross indicator with resolved path. (c) Show provider type. (d) 'Test Connection' button. (e) Auto-fallback to heuristic if unavailable.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Auto strategy triggers availability check. Resolved command displayed. Test button verifies. Unavailable shows warning + fallback.
sequentialTasks.completionNotes: Added claudeCheckStatus/claudeResolvedInfo/claudeCheckError to model state. Selecting 'auto' triggers tea.tick('check-claude') → handleClaudeCheck() creates temporary ClaudeCodeExecutor, calls resolve(). Available: green badge + command/provider. Unavailable: error badge + message + auto-fallback to heuristic. Test Connection button re-triggers check. Focus system updated: test-claude button at index 3 (conditional). Both keyboard (handleFocusActivate) and mouse (handleScreenMouseClick) paths trigger check. Guard for undefined prSplitConfig in tests. All tests pass.

sequentialTasks.task: T11: Wire post-split verification display into TUI execution screen
sequentialTasks.description: verifySplits() called but UI display minimal. Enhance: (a) Per-branch verification status as it completes. (b) Collect output in s.branchErrors. (c) 'Show Output' expandable per failed branch. (d) Equiv check after all verifications. (e) Update viewVerificationScreen.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Each branch shows progress. Pass=checkmark. Fail=cross with summary. Expandable output. Equiv after all verify.
sequentialTasks.completionNotes: Added tick-based per-branch verification (runVerifyBranch) between branch creation and equiv check. Model state: verificationResults[], verifyingIdx, verifyOutput{}, expandedVerifyBranch. runExecutionStep now 3 steps: branches→verify→equiv. viewExecutionScreen enhanced with 'Verifying Branches' section showing per-branch ✓/✗/▶/○ with duration, dependency skip detection, expandable output for failures (zone-based Show/Hide Output), and summary badges. Mouse expand/collapse in handleScreenMouseClick.

sequentialTasks.task: T12: Build CaptureSession in termmux — embedded command output capture
sequentialTasks.description: General-purpose CaptureSession: (a) Struct with cmd, pty, vterm, mutex. (b) Start/IsRunning/Output/Interrupt/Kill/Resize/Wait methods. (c) Output returns vterm Screenshot. (d) No pr-split code.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: CaptureSession exported. echo test works. IsRunning correct. Interrupt/Kill work. All platforms. No pr-split code in termmux.
sequentialTasks.completionNotes: CaptureSession struct in capture.go with Start/IsRunning/Output/Screen/Interrupt/Kill/Resize/Wait/Close/Write/SendEOF/ExitCode/Pid/Done. Background readerLoop feeds PTY→VTerm. JS bindings in module.go: newCaptureSession factory + WrapCaptureSession. 25 tests covering echo, IsRunning lifecycle, Interrupt, Kill, context cancel, Resize, double-Start, Close idempotent, PID, exit code, Write/SendEOF, Screen, multiline output, Done channel, concurrent output, env vars, working dir, default/custom dimensions, invalid command, not-started guards, start-after-close.

sequentialTasks.task: T13: Build osm:termmux JS bindings for CaptureSession
sequentialTasks.description: Expose CaptureSession to JS: (a) newCaptureSession factory. (b) JS methods: start/isRunning/output/interrupt/kill/resize/wait. (c) Event queuing. (d) Context cleanup. (e) Thread-safe.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: JS usage works. Events fire. Unit tests pass. No pr-split code.
sequentialTasks.completionNotes: JS bindings implemented in T12 (combined delivery — deadcode checker requires wiring). newCaptureSession(cmd, args?, opts?) factory and WrapCaptureSession both in module.go. Full API: start/isRunning/output/screen/interrupt/kill/resize/wait/write/sendEOF/close/pid/exitCode/isDone.

sequentialTasks.completionNotes: startVerifySession/cleanupVerifyWorktree in 06_verification.js. runVerifyBranch now launches CaptureSession (falls back to blocking verifySplit on Windows). pollVerifySession polls isDone() every 100ms. Live bordered viewport in viewExecutionScreen with auto-scroll, manual scroll (↑↓/wheel/Home/End), Ctrl+C/click interrupt, elapsed timer. Output freezes on done. 7 new model state fields.

sequentialTasks.completionNotes: Split-view toggle via Ctrl+L. Claude screenshot polled at 500ms via tick. Wizard top + Claude bottom with adjustable ratio (Ctrl+=/Ctrl+-). Focus switch via Tab. Independent scroll (↑↓/Home/End/wheel). Bordered Claude pane with placeholder when no session. 5 new model state fields. Pane divider with focus indicator.

sequentialTasks.description: Checkpoint system: (a) Save on state transition. (b) Offer resume on startup. (c) Includes state/plan/analysis/progress/errors. (d) Delete on completion. (e) Session lock for concurrency.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Cancel then restart offers Resume. Crash same. Completion deletes. Start Fresh available. Lock prevents concurrent.

continuousVerification.description: Execute `make` which runs build, lint (vet, staticcheck, betteralign, deadcode), and test with race detection. ALL must pass on ALL platforms (ubuntu, windows, macos). Use `make test` for fast feedback, `make` for full verification before commit.
continuousVerification.failPolicy: If ANY check fails, STOP IMMEDIATELY and fix before proceeding.
continuousVerification.testCoverage: Verify coverage for new code using `make cover`. Target >=90% for TUI functions, 100% for state machine transitions.

finalEnforcementProtocol.alert: ALL checks must pass. NEVER commit with failing checks.
Rule of Two MANDATORY before every commit. Two contiguous issue-free reviews of the exact same diff.
JS runs in Goja event loop. Never call sync subprocess operations in update(). Use tea.tick().
